clusters.title: Artemis II astronauts welcomed home to Houston after historic moonshot
clusters.neutral_headline: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
bullet_summary: Mission commander Reid Wiseman reported that the crew members, himself along with Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen, were "stable" and "green"
Reported by CBS News, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Artemis II astronauts welcomed home to Houston after historic moonshot
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts flew back to the Johnson Space Center in Houston Saturday to cheers and applause from family members and hundreds of NASA workers.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts flew back to the Johnson Space Center in Houston Saturday to cheers and applause from family members and hundreds of NASA workers.

items.title: Artemis II crew returns to Houston after successful mission
items.standfirst: The Artemis II astronauts spoke following the successful completion of their mission. They splashed down on Friday night off the coast of San Diego. Retired Lieutenant Colonel David Mahan joins CBS News to discuss.
items.content: The Artemis II astronauts spoke following the successful completion of their mission. They splashed down on Friday night off the coast of San Diego. Retired Lieutenant Colonel David Mahan joins CBS News to discuss.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts speak publicly for first time since successful moon mission
items.standfirst: The Artemis II crew spoke publicly for the first time after their successful mission at the Ellington Field Joint Reserve Base in Houston, Texas.
items.content: The Artemis II crew spoke publicly for the first time after their successful mission at the Ellington Field Joint Reserve Base in Houston, Texas.

items.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
items.standfirst: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.
items.content: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

items.title: Artemis II crew splashes down safely
items.standfirst: The NASA spacecraft carrying four astronauts splashed down as planned Friday off the California coast, capping the US space agency's successful crewed test mission around the Moon, the first such flyby in more than 50 years. Mission commander Reid Wiseman reported that the crew members, himself along with Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen, were "stable" and "green". Senior Lecturer at Center for Hypersonics, Dr. Christ James, shares some more insights on the mission and landing.
items.content: The NASA spacecraft carrying four astronauts splashed down as planned Friday off the California coast, capping the US space agency's successful crewed test mission around the Moon, the first such flyby in more than 50 years. Mission commander Reid Wiseman reported that the crew members, himself along with Christina Koch, Victor Glover and Jeremy Hansen, were "stable" and "green". Senior Lecturer at Center for Hypersonics, Dr. Christ James, shares some more insights on the mission and landing.

items.title: First lunar flyby in over 50 years hailed as perfect mission
items.standfirst: NASA celebrated late Friday as four astronauts splashed down safely off the California coast, completing the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years and marking a major milestone for the US space agency’s ambitions to return humans to the moon. France 24 correspondent Wassim Cornet was in Diego, reporting the moment of the landing at the AirSpace Museum.
items.content: NASA celebrated late Friday as four astronauts splashed down safely off the California coast, completing the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years and marking a major milestone for the US space agency’s ambitions to return humans to the moon. France 24 correspondent Wassim Cornet was in Diego, reporting the moment of the landing at the AirSpace Museum.

items.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
items.standfirst: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.
items.content: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

items.title: Record-breaking Artemis II mission ends with 'perfect splashdown' in Pacific Ocean
items.standfirst: Artemis II's history-making astronauts have successfully arrived back on Earth with a "perfect bullseye splashdown" off the coast of California.
items.content: Artemis II's history-making astronauts have successfully arrived back on Earth with a "perfect bullseye splashdown" off the coast of California.

items.title: Artemis II: Return to the Moon
items.standfirst: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Venturing further from Earth than ever before.
items.content: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Venturing further from Earth than ever before.

items.title: Artemis II Safely Returns to Earth After Historic Moon Mission
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis crew of four astronauts returned to Earth and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, capping humanity’s first return to the moon in more than 50 years. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA’s Artemis crew of four astronauts returned to Earth and splashed down in the Pacific Ocean, capping humanity’s first return to the moon in more than 50 years. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Artemis II Astronauts Safely Return to Earth After Historic Flight Around the Moon
items.standfirst: After traveling a greater distance from Earth than any humans before them, the astronauts of Artemis II have safely returned home.
items.content: After traveling a greater distance from Earth than any humans before them, the astronauts of Artemis II have safely returned home.

items.title: NASA’s Artemis Crew Set to Splashdown After Moon Voyage
items.standfirst: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: NASA’s Artemis II crew prepares for a splashdown after a historic trip around the moon. Laurie Leshin, former head of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, discusses the risk the astronauts face on their return to Earth and what comes next for the Artemis program. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: How and When to Watch the Artemis II Mission’s Return to Earth
items.standfirst: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.
items.content: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.

clusters.title: Hungarians vote in a key election that could unseat populist Prime Minister Orbán
clusters.neutral_headline: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, NPR News, NBC World

items.title: Hungarians vote in a key election that could unseat populist Prime Minister Orbán
items.standfirst: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing from US President Donald Trump.
items.content: Hungarians headed to the polls on Sunday in a pivotal parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year grip on power, as the opposition led by Peter Magyar challenges his nationalist rule in a contest drawing scrutiny across Europe and backing from US President Donald Trump.

items.title: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
items.standfirst: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.
items.content: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

items.title: After 16 years in power, Putin's closest friend in Europe faces a pivotal election
items.standfirst: Few leaders have done more than Hungary’s Viktor Orbán to shape the global right-wing populism that informed President Donald Trump’s MAGA project.
items.content: Few leaders have done more than Hungary’s Viktor Orbán to shape the global right-wing populism that informed President Donald Trump’s MAGA project.

items.title: An Orban loss in Hungary’s election could be the turning point Putin fears
items.standfirst: Hungary's legislative elections on Sunday are being closely watched in Moscow. Prime Minister Viktor Orban has long been Russian President Vladimir Putin's closest ally within the European Union, and a victory for the opposition Tisza party led by Peter Magyar could undermine the Kremlin's influence in the bloc.
items.content: Hungary's legislative elections on Sunday are being closely watched in Moscow. Prime Minister Viktor Orban has long been Russian President Vladimir Putin's closest ally within the European Union, and a victory for the opposition Tisza party led by Peter Magyar could undermine the Kremlin's influence in the bloc.

items.title: Orbán and Magyar hold rival rallies in Hungary ahead of Sunday's elections
items.standfirst: Voters in Hungary go to the polls tomorrow, with a high-profile American endorsement looming over the campaign. Donald Trump has made no secret of his support for Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, posting on social media urging Hungarians to vote for him, while Vice President JD Vance spent two days in Budapest this week effectively campaigning on his behalf. It is part of a broader pattern: Trump has openly backed candidates in elections across Latin America, Asia and Eastern Europe, breaking with a long-standing US tradition of staying out of other countries’ politics. Charlotte Lam reports.
items.content: Voters in Hungary go to the polls tomorrow, with a high-profile American endorsement looming over the campaign. Donald Trump has made no secret of his support for Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, posting on social media urging Hungarians to vote for him, while Vice President JD Vance spent two days in Budapest this week effectively campaigning on his behalf. It is part of a broader pattern: Trump has openly backed candidates in elections across Latin America, Asia and Eastern Europe, breaking with a long-standing US tradition of staying out of other countries’ politics. Charlotte Lam reports.

clusters.title: Russia and Ukraine trade strikes before temporary ceasefire
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia and Ukraine trade strikes before temporary ceasefire
bullet_summary: The Kremlin said it had ordered a 32-hour truce from 4pm (13:00 GMT) to midnight on Sunday, a week after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky proposed a halt to hostilities for the holiday
Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported
KYIV, Ukraine — Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported, ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter

items.title: Russia and Ukraine trade strikes before temporary ceasefire
items.standfirst: An Orthodox Easter ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine began on Saturday. The Kremlin said it had ordered a 32-hour truce from 4pm (13:00 GMT) to midnight on Sunday, a week after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky proposed a halt to hostilities for the holiday. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv with the latest updates.
items.content: An Orthodox Easter ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine began on Saturday. The Kremlin said it had ordered a 32-hour truce from 4pm (13:00 GMT) to midnight on Sunday, a week after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky proposed a halt to hostilities for the holiday. France 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv with the latest updates.

items.title: Russian drone strikes kill 2 in Ukraine ahead of holiday ceasefire

items.title: Russian strikes on Odesa kill 2 ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
items.standfirst: KYIV, Ukraine — Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported, ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter
items.content: KYIV, Ukraine — Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian city of Odesa overnight into Saturday, local authorities reported, ahead of a proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter

items.title: Russia, Ukraine to begin Orthodox Easter truce
items.standfirst: A pause in fighting in the Ukraine war was set for Sunday, but it does not appear to be taking hold. Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa overnight, just hours before a proposed Easter ceasefire was due to come into effect. Russian President Vladimir Putin has declared a 32-hour halt to hostilities through the end of Sunday. Kyiv says it is ready to reciprocate, but previous ceasefire attempts have gone nowhere, with both sides accusing each other of violations. Catherine Viette reports.
items.content: A pause in fighting in the Ukraine war was set for Sunday, but it does not appear to be taking hold. Russian drone strikes killed at least two people in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa overnight, just hours before a proposed Easter ceasefire was due to come into effect. Russian President Vladimir Putin has declared a 32-hour halt to hostilities through the end of Sunday. Kyiv says it is ready to reciprocate, but previous ceasefire attempts have gone nowhere, with both sides accusing each other of violations. Catherine Viette reports.

clusters.title: Cyber Security In Focus As Bank CEO's Race to DC
clusters.neutral_headline: Cyber Security In Focus As Bank CEO's Race to DC
bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Guardian Business

items.title: Cyber Security In Focus As Bank CEO's Race to DC
items.standfirst: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to give them an urgent warning: an artificial intelligence tool from Anthropic PBC marks the beginning of a new era of cybersecurity. The April 7 meeting in Washington was focused on Mythos, a new AI model that Anthropic says is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can only be released to a limited number of carefully-chosen parties. If tools like Mythos fall into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could provide attackers with a powerful new weapon to steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure. Bloomberg's Todd Gillespie joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Why Officials Are So Worried About Mythos, Anthropic’s New AI

items.title: Wall Street CEOs Summoned to Discuss Anthropic AI Risks | Bloomberg Tech 4/10/2026
items.standfirst: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde discusses concerns about cyber risks raised by Anthropic’s newest AI model. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting to discuss the threat. Plus, CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator discusses deals with Anthropic and Meta as demand for compute shows no sign of slowing. And, after a record breaking trip around the moon the crew of Artemis II prepares to splash down in the Pacific ocean. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde discusses concerns about cyber risks raised by Anthropic’s newest AI model. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to an urgent meeting to discuss the threat. items.title: US summons bank bosses over cyber risks from Anthropic’s latest AI model
items.standfirst: Fed chair Jerome Powell reportedly attends meeting in Washington following release of Claude MythosThe US Treasury secretary, Scott Bessent, summoned major American bank chiefs to a meeting in Washington this week amid concerns over the cyber risks posed by Anthropic’s latest AI model, according to reports.Jerome Powell, chair of the Rederal Reserve, was said to have been among those gathered at the Treasury headquarters for the meeting after the release of the Claude Mythos AI model that Anthropic says poses unprecedented cybersecurity risks. Continue reading...
